title: Task packs #3–#5 across ≥ 2 regions → mapped + validated + merged
context: M2 requires scaling throughput to at least 5 packs validated and merged cumulatively across at least 2 distinct regions, demonstrating the pipeline works beyond a single area. Each pack uses the QA tool (validation-013), follows the import playbook (import-playbook-014), and is measured against the validator-time efficiency baseline established in M0/M1. The goal is measurable validator-time reduction with no drop in pass rate.
objective: Assemble, execute, and document three additional task packs (#3–#5) using the full M1–M2 toolchain across at least 2 distinct regions, each reaching a validated, merged outcome.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of the three packs covers a distinct confirmed partner area, passing both imagery-gate-002 and harm-gate-003 with committed artifacts.
Packs span at least 2 distinct geographic regions.
Each pack uses the QA/validation tool (validation-013) during the mapping phase to produce a human-fixable worklist before validator sign-off.
Each pack follows the import-playbook-014 governance decision before any bulk contribution.
Each pack's effort is registered as OSMF Organised Editing; features are mapped by humans, validated, and merged into OSM (not reverted); outcomes recorded per pack.
Validator-time per 100 validated features is recorded; median improves versus the M0/M1 baseline with no drop in pass rate.
Reviewed by the licensing/imagery reviewer, do-no-harm reviewer, and experienced OSM validator per pack.
output: Three committed task packs (#3–#5) with gate artifacts and channel artifacts, plus three outcomes/<pack-id>.json files recording validated-feature counts, pass rates, validator-time metrics, and changeset/TM references across at least 2 distinct regions.
